slides.title: FastAPI Essentials
slides.subtitle:
High Performance, Type-Safe, and Asynchronous APIs
Modern Python Web Development at Scale

slides.name: Aravind Govindhasamy
slides.title: Software Developer
slides.experience: Specialist in Python, .NET, and Scalable Backend Systems
slides.oss_experience: Open Source Contributor & Technical Content Creator

box.content: Built on the shoulders of giants: Starlette and Pydantic.
A high-performance web framework for building APIs with Python 3.8+ based on standard Python type hints.

list.text: Fast: Very high performance, on par with NodeJS and Go (thanks to Starlette).
Type-Safe: Based on Python 3.8+ type hints for best editor support.
Robust: Production-ready code with automatic interactive documentation.

box.content: FastAPI isn't just a framework; it's an orchestration of modern Python standards.

list.text: Pydantic v2: Data validation and settings management using python type annotations.
Starlette: A lightweight ASGI framework/toolkit, which is ideal for building high performance asyncio services.
Uvicorn: A lightning-fast ASGI server implementation, using uvloop and httptools.

box.content: Pydantic v2 is rewritten in Rust, making it up to 20x faster than v1.

list.text: Rust Core: Logic is offloaded to a high-performance Rust engine.
Strict Mode: Enforce exact types without implicit conversion.
JSON Schema: Native extraction of OpenAPI-compliant schemas.

box.content: FastAPI uses standard Python types to define the structure of your data.
box.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel

class Item(BaseModel):
    name: str
    price: float
    is_offer: bool | None = None

box.content: Using Annotated to add extra metadata to your type hints for FastAPI and Pydantic.
box.code:
from typing import Annotated
from fastapi import Query

@app.get("/")
async def read_items(q: Annotated[str | None, Query(max_length=50)] = None): ...

box.content: Use @field_validator to add custom logic to your data models.
The logic behind your API endpoints.
Binding logic to URLs using standard HTTP methods.
FastAPI uses Pydantic models to automatically validate incoming JSON bodies.
Declare parameters and their types directly in the function signature.

list.text: Path Parameters: Extracted from the URL path.
Query Parameters: Extracted from the URL query string.
Validation: Use Query and Path classes for extra metadata.

box.content: Zero-effort API specifications.
FastAPI generates a standard OpenAPI schema and serves a dedicated Swagger UI at /docs.

list.text: Live Testing: Send requests directly from the browser.
Auto-Discovery: Every endpoint, model, and parameter is documented.

box.content: An alternative, clean documentation format available at /redoc, perfect for developers.
Shared logic made simple.
FastAPI has an extremely powerful but simple Dependency Injection system.
Why use Dependency Injection?

list.text: Code Reuse: Share database sessions, security, and more.
Testability: Easily override dependencies with mocks during testing.
Hierarchy: Dependencies can depend on other dependencies.

box.content: Dependencies can depend on other dependencies, creating a powerful tree of shared logic.
Concurrency vs. Parallelism.
FastAPI supports async def natively. This is the key to its high performance.
box.code:
@app.get("/")
async def read_results():
    results = await some_async_db_call()
    return results

box.content: FastAPI uses 'anyio' to handle both async def and regular def functions optimally.

list.text: Async Def: Runs in the main event loop (I/O non-blocking).
Regular Def: Runs in an external threadpool (safely blocking).

box.content: OAuth2, Scopes, and JWT mastery.
FastAPI provides built-in tools for OAuth2, including integrated Swagger login.
Secure your API with JSON Web Tokens. Decouple your auth logic from your business logic.

list.text: Signing: Use PyJWT or python-jose to sign tokens.
Verification: Automatic token validation in your dependencies.

box.content: Granular permissions using OAuth2 Scopes. Define what each user can see and do.
box.code:
async def get_current_user(
    security_scopes: SecurityScopes, 
    token: str = Depends(oauth2_scheme)
): ...

box.content: SQLAlchemy, Tortoise, and Async ORMs.
FastAPI pairs perfectly with SQLAlchemy's new asynchronous API.
box.code:
async_engine = create_async_engine(DATABASE_URL)
AsyncSessionLocal = sessionmaker(
    async_engine, class_=AsyncSession, expire_on_commit=False
)

box.content: Use Alembic to manage your database schema changes over time.
box.code:
# shell: alembic revision --autogenerate -m "Initial"
# shell: alembic upgrade head

box.content: An easy-to-use asyncio ORM inspired by Django, designed specifically for FastAPI/Starlette.
box.code: await Tortoise.init(db_url="sqlite://db.sqlite3")

box.content: Lifecycle events and background work.
Define logic that should run before the application starts or after it finishes.
box.code:
@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    # Startup logic (e.g., connect to DB)
    yield
    # Shutdown logic (e.g., disconnect)

box.content: Execute heavy tasks after returning a response, without blocking the client.
Intercept every request and response to add headers, logging, or timing info.
box.code:
@app.middleware("http")
async def add_process_time_header(request, call_next):
    start_time = time.time()
    response = await call_next(request)
    return response

box.content: Middlewares are executed in reverse order of how they were added (LIFO) for the request, and then forward for the response.

list.text: Request: Bottom to Top.
Response: Top to Bottom.

box.content: Scaling FastAPI in the real world.
For production, use Gunicorn with the Uvicorn worker class for multi-process scalability.
box.code: # shell: gunicorn -w 4 -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker main:app

box.content: Standardize your deployment with multi-stage Docker builds for minimal image size.
box.code:
FROM python:3.11-slim
COPY . /app
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt
CMD ["uvicorn", "main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0"]

box.content: FastAPI is ideal for Kubernetes and Serverless (AWS Lambda, Google Cloud Run).

list.text: Liveness/Readiness: Easy healthcheck endpoints.
Stateless: Perfect for horizontal scaling.

box.content: Fast, reliable, and type-safe tests.
Built on 'httpx', the TestClient allows you to test your API without running a server.
box.code:
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
client = TestClient(app)

def test_read_main():
    response = client.get("/")
    assert response.status_code == 200

box.content: Swap out real databases or external services for mocks during testing effortlessly.
box.code: app.dependency_overrides[get_db] = override_get_db

box.content: Use 'pytest-asyncio' and 'httpx.AsyncClient' for testing async endpoints.
box.code:
async with AsyncClient(app=app) as ac:
    response = await ac.get("/")

box.content: Architecting for complexity.
Organize code by feature, not by layer. Keep all logic for a feature in one place.

list.text: Modules: users/, orders/, products/.
Decoupling: Use APIRouters to separate concerns.

box.content: Why FastAPI is the go-to for modern distributed systems.

list.text: Lightweight: Low memory footprint.
Communication: Native support for JSON and gRPC (via extensions).

box.content: The future of Python web APIs.
FastAPI brings performance, type-safety, and developer joy to Python.

list.text: Production-ready security and documentation.
Asynchronous by default.
Built on modern Python standards.

box.content: Any questions? Thank you for attending!
